### INFO (3)

[INFO] Auto-resolved: SPEC > PRD on ConfirmDialog surface color
  Note: README.md (PRD) states that light/dark theming uses var(--cream) /
    var(--ink) token pairs for flipping surfaces. ds-confirmdialog.jsx (SPEC)
    specifies ConfirmDialog background as rgba(255,255,255,.97) — a hardcoded
    near-white, not var(--cream). SPEC wins per precedence. The dialog is
    intentionally always-light inside the overlay backdrop (maximum contrast).
    This is consistent with the PRD's own DECISION-004 "always-dark surfaces
    use hardcoded values" rule applied symmetrically to an always-light element.
  Winner: ds-confirmdialog.jsx (SPEC)
  source (PRD): /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md
  source (SPEC): /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-confirmdialog.jsx
  Logged in constraints.md as CONSTRAINT-010.

[INFO] --amber-vivid token value inferred from DataGrid spec, not explicitly
  declared in README token table
  Note: ds-datagrid.jsx (SPEC) references var(--amber-vivid) for medium-priority
    dot color. The README token table (PRD) lists --blue-vivid, --purple-vivid,
    --green-vivid, --red-vivid explicitly but does not list --amber-vivid by name.
    The implied value is #f59e0b (same as --amber in light mode). Synthesized intel
    adds --amber-vivid to CONSTRAINT-001 as a required token with value #f59e0b.
    If this is incorrect, update the token value before the design token pass runs.
  source (SPEC): /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-datagrid.jsx
  source (PRD): /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md
